items.title: MOH Issuing New Circular On Junior Medical Officers’ Working Hours
items.summary: Dr Dzul says the Health DG will issue a new circular on junior medical officers' working hours, besides stressing compliance with flexi-work guidelines for housemen. Junior doctors in Malaysia work 65 to 85 hours weekly, far higher than the UK's 40 hours. The post MOH Issuing New Circular On Junior Medical Officers’ Working Hours appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Mudah Healthtech, Sihat XPress Kiosk To Accelerate Preventive Care
items.summary: Mudah Healthtech’s Sihat Xpress kiosk closes the health care gap between urban and rural areas, acting as a critical health care link. The post Mudah Healthtech, Sihat XPress Kiosk To Accelerate Preventive Care appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Sustainability Of Primary Care Supersedes Doctors’ Income: FPMPAM
items.summary: FPMPAM says MMA's survey about GP clinics appears to refer to clinic business revenue, rather than doctors' personal income, adding that the bigger issue is about the sustainability of Malaysia's community-based primary care system, not doctors' earnings. The post Sustainability Of Primary Care Supersedes Doctors’ Income: FPMPAM appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: ‘Godzilla’ El Niño May Slash Malaysia’s Rice Output
items.summary: Extreme weather projections could slash local rice production from ~40% contribution in key granary states to as low as 20%, says a KPKM official. Malaysia only produces 52% of its rice needs, leaving it exposed if exporting countries restrict supply. The post ‘Godzilla’ El Niño May Slash Malaysia’s Rice Output appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Newcastle University Admits Malaysia Medical Graduates Deprioritised For UK Training
items.summary: Newcastle University admits that medical graduates at its Malaysian campus, NUMed, have been deprioritised for training in the NHS, following the UK's new Medical Training (Prioritisation) Act, but claims that NUMed's MBBS degree is still GMC accredited. The post Newcastle University Admits Malaysia Medical Graduates Deprioritised For UK Training appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Jaya Grocer Launches ‘JS Cage-Free Eggs’ To Advance Animal Welfare
items.summary: With the introduction of JS Cage-Free Eggs, Jaya Grocer reinforces its role as a forward-thinking retailer committed to innovation, quality, and responsibility. The post Jaya Grocer Launches ‘JS Cage-Free Eggs’ To Advance Animal Welfare appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Junior Doctors Work 10 To 15 Overtime Hours Weekly Without Pay: MMI
items.summary: MMI says junior doctors in Malaysia work 10-15 unrecorded and unpaid overtime hours weekly. HOs or MOs in Malaysia average at 65-85 weekly working hours, compared to 40 hours in the UK. MMI urges MOH to formalise a Safe Working Hours Policy for doctors. The post Junior Doctors Work 10 To 15 Overtime Hours Weekly Without Pay: MMI appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Galen Centre Moots Health Service Commission With Ring-Fenced Five-Year Funding
items.summary: The Galen Centre calls for the establishment of a Health Service Commission (SPK) authorised to create and fill positions, and set salary scales, with funding for these positions ring-fenced in a 5-year fiscal framework. MPs have mixed views about the SPK. The post Galen Centre Moots Health Service Commission With Ring-Fenced Five-Year Funding appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Post-Maternity Wage Allowance In Malaysia: Breastfeeding, Work Retention, And Structural Conditions — June Joseph
items.summary: The return to work is where biological needs meet institutional design. If that interface is unsupported, even strong early breastfeeding initiation will struggle to continue. The post Post-Maternity Wage Allowance In Malaysia: Breastfeeding, Work Retention, And Structural Conditions — June Joseph appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Beyond Shadow AI: A Physician’s Blueprint For Clinical Sovereignty — Dr Lim Wan Chieh
items.summary: AI cannot replicate the lived, human insight of a physician. It is simply the loom; the physician remains the weaver. The post Beyond Shadow AI: A Physician’s Blueprint For Clinical Sovereignty — Dr Lim Wan Chieh appeared first on CodeBlue .
